mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or held at exceptionally low levels, equity discount rates stay compressed and valuation support lifts the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days. The signal is confirmed if lower policy rates precede positive forward returns and fails if rate declines do not improve 20-day SPX performance.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not higher than baseline after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the sign of the return relation is negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold marks weakening household income and risk appetite, which depresses cyclical earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be visible after an unemployment break, not during stable labor-market conditions.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the conditional mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous strengthening in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals improving payroll income and retail spending, which supports near-term earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. Either series alone is insufficient if the other remains weak.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS-RSAFS improvement does not outperform single-series signals, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive when both series are rising together.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is accelerating while HOUST remains weak, the mix points to factory-led output growth without an overheating housing channel, which can support risk assets over the next 60 trading days through better production and margin expectations. The hypothesis fails if industrial acceleration is not rewarded when housing is subdued.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive while HOUST 3-month change stays below zero
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO-up/HOUST-down regime does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns, or if returns are indistinguishable from unconditional averages.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 growth indicates slowing aggregate demand and weaker forward earnings, which should weigh on the S&P 500 over the next 60 trading days. The relation is strongest when GDP growth is below its recent trend and fails if slowing GDP does not precede equity underperformance.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth falls below its 4-quarter average
failure_condition: Reject if GDP slowdowns are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the conditional mean return remains above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in CPIAUCSL inflation combined with a non-easing FEDFUNDS stance compresses real rates and raises policy uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is active only when inflation is accelerating faster than policy accommodation.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS
regime_hint: inflation shock with passive policy response
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-above-policy episodes are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS count is an early sign of payroll contraction, which reduces expected consumption and corporate revenue and should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next 5 trading days. The signal is a short-horizon labor shock and should not work when payrolls are stable.
failure_condition: Reject if negative PAYEMS changes are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is not better than random over rolling evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS rises while HOUST also rises, consumer spending and housing demand are both improving, which should support earnings breadth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The joint condition matters because broad demand strength, not either series alone, drives the signal.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST gains do not lead to above-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero.
